TSLA: A Tesla Owner Spent $73 to Sue Tesla. He Just Walked Away With $10,600. A Tesla owner successfully sued Tesla in small claims court for $10,600 after paying $73 in fees. The lawsuit stemmed from the false promise of Full Self-Driving (FSD) capabilities, which the owner argued never reached the Level 5 autonomy Elon Musk advertised. This case highlights a potential precedent for other FSD purchasers seeking refunds, especially since Tesla's legal team offered little defense.

Toyota Is Taking The Fight To BMW And Tesla, But Not With Cars. Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada (TMMC) is implementing humanoid robots named Digit from Agility Robotics in its Ontario plants, joining BMW and Tesla in using advanced robotics for manufacturing. These robots will handle repetitive, physically demanding tasks, allowing human workers to be reassigned to more value-added roles. This move aims to improve efficiency and worker safety, though concerns about job displacement are also noted.

Teslas Among Fastest-Selling Used Cars, Depreciate Less Than EV Rivals. A new study reveals that Tesla vehicles are among the quickest to sell on the used car market and depreciate significantly less than their non-Tesla EV rivals. This trend comes as demand for hybrids has also increased, suggesting a shift in consumer preferences in the used vehicle market. Despite a drop in overall EV market share in California, Tesla's Model Y remains the best-selling vehicle in the state.

UBS Group AG Decreases Holdings in Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares $TSLL. UBS Group AG significantly reduced its stake in Direxion Daily TSLA Bull 2X Shares (NASDAQ:TSLL) by 34.3% in the fourth quarter, selling over 2.7 million shares and now holding 5.21 million shares valued at approximately $99.6 million. The leveraged ETF, which provides 1.5x exposure to Tesla stock, recently announced a quarterly dividend of $0.0994. Despite UBS's reduction, several other investment firms either increased their holdings or initiated new stakes in TSLL.

Elon Musk's Robotaxi Plans Take Shape With Dallas, Houston Rollout, But Tesla Is Still Far Behind Waymo. Tesla has expanded its unsupervised Robotaxi operations to Dallas and Houston, adding to its existing Austin fleet, bringing the total to 25 unsupervised vehicles across these three Texas cities. Despite this growth, the company remains significantly behind Alphabet Inc.'s Waymo in terms of fleet size, service area, and completed rides, as Waymo operates in 10 cities and completes 500,000 rides weekly. Other companies like Uber, WeRide, and Baidu's Apollo Go are also making strides in the global robotaxi market through partnerships and expanded services.

Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Apple, Intel, Tesla — And Markets Extend April Rally. US stock markets extended their April rally, driven by optimism around AI infrastructure spending and strong company earnings from tech and semiconductor firms. This article highlights several bullish and bearish calls on key stocks including Apple, Intel, Tesla, Amazon, AMD, Bloom Energy, Enphase Energy, Roblox, and Clorox, detailing their recent performance and contributing factors. It also touches on concerns regarding inflation persistence and engineered market sentiment, especially concerning Tesla.

Tesla Semi production ramps up with first vehicle off high-volume line. Tesla has announced that its first Semi truck has rolled off the high-volume production line at Gigafactory Nevada, marking a significant milestone after its initial unveiling in 2017. The electric truck, boasting a 500-mile range model costing $300,000 and a 300-mile range version at $260,000, is now entering operational pilots with companies like MDB Transportation for last-mile container moves. Additionally, infrastructure for the Semi is expanding, with Pilot Travel Centers partnering to install Megachargers at truck stops across major interstates, and Tesla actively recruiting talent to support commercial operations and manufacturing in Texas.

Tesla Got $573 Million from SpaceX and xAI in 2025. Tesla generated over half a billion dollars in revenue in 2025 by selling products to Elon Musk's other companies, SpaceX and xAI. The bulk of this, $430.1 million, came from xAI purchasing Tesla Megapacks, while SpaceX contributed $143.3 million, reportedly including Cybertruck sales. This financial interchange highlights the intertwined operations and potential strategic collaborations between Musk's various ventures, even as Tesla faces challenges like Cybertruck sales.

Roundhill TSLA WeeklyPay ETF To Go Ex-Dividend On May 4th, 2026 With 0.12047 USD Dividend Per Share. The Roundhill TSLA WeeklyPay ETF (NYSEARCA:TSLW) will go ex-dividend on May 4th, 2026. Shareholders recorded on this date will receive a dividend of 0.12047 USD per share. This announcement is significant for investors tracking dividend distributions from this ETF.

Cathie Wood’s ARK Invest adds BitMine shares as it offloads $30M in Tesla stock. Cathie Wood's ARK Invest has strategically acquired shares in BitMine (OTCQB: BTMN) while simultaneously selling off a significant portion of its Tesla (NASDAQ: TSLA) stock, valued at $30 million. This move suggests a shifting investment focus within ARK's portfolio.

Tesla, Nvidia, Intel: April's Most-Searched Tickers Revealed - Amazon.com (NASDAQ:AMZN), Avis Budget Gr. Benzinga Pro has released its list of the most-searched tickers for April, highlighting continued strong interest in technology stocks, particularly semiconductors. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) remained the top-searched ticker, while Tesla (TSLA) surged to second place. Intel (INTC) and SanDisk (SNDK) rejoined the top 10 after significant year-to-date gains and increased search interest.

Tesla, Rivian, or Lucid: Which EV Stock Came Out Ahead in April?. In April, Rivian (RIVN) stock outperformed both Tesla (TSLA) and Lucid Group (LCID), surging 9% due to a $1 billion Volkswagen investment and a $4.5 billion Department of Energy loan, alongside strong Q1 earnings. Tesla saw a modest 3% gain, while Lucid Group experienced a significant 33% decline following a large Q4 EPS miss and negative free cash flow, highlighting the varied performance of EV stocks influenced by scale, execution, and cash burn.

Tesla, Atlassian among market cap stock movers on Friday. Friday's market saw significant stock movements, with Tesla and Atlassian among the companies experiencing notable changes in their market cap. Oracle and Atlassian rallied due to positive news, while Roblox and Summit Therapeutics faced declines. The article details these movers across mega-cap, large-cap, mid-cap, and small-cap categories.

Tesla reports Elon Musk compensation at $158 billion for 2025. Tesla reported Elon Musk's compensation for 2025 at $158 billion, but he did not realize this amount because performance targets were not met. Musk draws no salary from Tesla. The article also touches on related automotive news, including April U.S. auto sales declines and updates on tariffs and EV cancellations.

Musk teases Bill Gates as the first Tesla Semi rolls off the volume production line at Giga Nevada. Tesla has officially begun volume production of its Semi-truck at Giga Nevada, an achievement marked by CEO Elon Musk with a light-hearted taunt directed at Bill Gates. This jab stems from a past conversation where Gates had expressed skepticism about the feasibility of long-range electric semi-trucks, despite Tesla already having them in operation with companies like Pepsi. The article highlights Tesla's continued innovation and the commencement of commercial production for the highly anticipated electric truck.
TGT: Strs Ohio Sells 5,298 Shares of Ulta Beauty Inc. $ULTA. Strs Ohio has reduced its holdings in Ulta Beauty Inc. by 10.1%, selling 5,298 shares in the 4th quarter and now owning 47,282 shares valued at $28.6 million. This comes amidst other institutional investors adjusting their stakes in Ulta, and a director also selling 452 shares. The stock has received a "Moderate Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $674.23, despite competitive pressures from Walmart and the end of its Target partnership.

Robertson Stephens Wealth Management LLC Buys 30,254 Shares of Target Corporation $TGT. Robertson Stephens Wealth Management LLC significantly increased its stake in Target Corporation, acquiring an additional 30,254 shares to hold a total of 34,497 shares valued at $3.37 million. This comes as Target reported strong quarterly earnings, beating estimates with solid revenue, and declared a quarterly dividend of $1.14 per share, indicating an attractive yield for investors. Meanwhile, other institutional investors like Vanguard Group and State Street Corp also adjusted their positions, and analysts have issued various ratings and price targets for TGT.

Artemis Investment Management LLP Reduces Holdings in Ulta Beauty Inc. $ULTA. Artemis Investment Management LLP reduced its stake in Ulta Beauty Inc. by 14.7% in the fourth quarter, selling 3,681 shares, while other institutional investors like Vanguard Group Inc. and State Street Corp. increased their holdings. Ulta Beauty reported stronger-than-expected quarterly results with EPS of $8.01 and revenue of $3.90 billion, also issuing FY2026 guidance of $28.05-$28.55 EPS. Analyst sentiment remains mixed but generally positive, with a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and a target price of $674.23, despite varied individual analyst ratings and a recent operational setback with the ending of the Target partnership.

SevenBridge Financial Group LLC Decreases Stock Holdings in Target Corporation $TGT. Teachers get 10% off at Target May 3-9, plus 50% off membership. Target (NYSE: TGT) is offering a 10% storewide discount to verified teachers who are members of Target Circle during Teacher Appreciation Week, May 3-9, 2026. Additionally, verified teachers can get over 50% off an annual Target Circle 360 membership and benefit from simplified verification and year-round offers. The company will also conduct local school activations in Denver and Las Vegas through its Bullseye Builds with Community Program to enhance shared spaces and outdoor learning areas.
SBUX: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Philip Morris International, Honeywell, Starbucks and United-Guardian. The reports discuss each company's recent performance, growth drivers, and challenges, providing an overview of their financial health and market position.

Why Starbucks (SBUX) Is Up 8.2% After Mixed Q2 Results And Higher Earnings Guidance. Starbucks reported mixed Q2 results with revenue up but net income for the first six months down year-over-year. Despite ongoing margin pressures from elevated labor and store investment costs, the company raised its full-year earnings guidance, indicating the effectiveness of its "Back to Starbucks" initiatives in boosting sales and customer traffic. The core challenge for Starbucks remains translating improved sales into sustainable margin recovery and managing cost pressures, which will be critical for its longer-term performance.

PFA Pension Forsikringsaktieselskab Takes Position in Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. PFA Pension Forsikringsaktieselskab recently acquired a new stake in Starbucks Corporation, purchasing over 700,000 shares valued at approximately $59.7 million during the fourth quarter. Several other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Starbucks, while company insiders made significant share sales in March. Starbucks reported strong earnings for the last quarter, beating analyst estimates, and announced a quarterly dividend, though some analysts warn about potential valuation issues after its recent stock surge.

SBUX - Starbucks Corp Stock Price and Quote. Nashville mayor considers cash incentives for Starbucks in rare move. Nashville Mayor Freddie O'Connell is reportedly considering offering cash incentives to Starbucks for its corporate expansion in Tennessee, a move that would represent a significant policy shift for his administration. O'Connell has previously avoided such cash-per-job incentives in an effort to attract high-paying jobs without negatively impacting local businesses. This potential deal highlights Nashville's ongoing effort to balance economic development with local business concerns.

Danske Bank A S Acquires 48,706 Shares of Starbucks Corporation $SBUX. Danske Bank A S increased its stake in Starbucks Corporation by 9.3% in the fourth quarter, purchasing an additional 48,706 shares and bringing their total ownership to 573,419 shares valued at $48.29 million. Other institutional investors also adjusted their positions in Starbucks, which saw positive sentiment from a Q2 earnings beat and improved margins, despite ongoing concerns about valuation and margin pressure. The company recently announced a quarterly dividend of $0.62 per share.

Hotels, Sustainability & Coffee: This Week’s Top Stories. This article highlights top news in the food and drink industry, focusing on sustainability efforts from major companies. Key stories include Liverpool FC's food sustainability certification, Anheuser-Busch's increased US manufacturing investment, Hershey's 10-year sustainability roadmap, Starbucks' new weekly pay structure for baristas, and easyJet's initiatives to reduce plastic and carbon emissions in hotels. These developments showcase a growing industry trend towards environmental responsibility and operational efficiency.

Starbucks Invested $500 Million in a Turnaround and Now Wall Street Is Drinking Up SBUX Stock. Starbucks invested over $500 million in a turnaround strategy called "Back to Starbucks," focusing on enhancing the in-store experience, increasing labor hours, and expanding store rosters. This investment has led to a stronger-than-expected fiscal Q2 2026 performance, causing a 9% stock jump and prompting Wall Street analysts to raise their price targets and maintain "Buy" ratings. The company is also expanding in China through a new joint venture and plans to open hundreds of new U.S. stores, signaling a promising path for continued growth.

Stock Market Today: Dow Jones, Nasdaq Futures Gain Ahead Of Powell's Last Speech As Fed Chair— Robinhood, Starbucks, Alphabet In Focus. U.S. stock futures rose ahead of what is anticipated to be Jerome Powell's final press conference as Fed Chair, with markets expecting interest rates to remain unchanged. Booking Holdings and Robinhood saw their shares drop after reporting earnings that missed estimates, while Starbucks jumped due to stronger-than-expected revenue. Amazon and Alphabet were also in focus as they prepared to release their earnings reports.

The Walt Disney Company (DIS): Navigating the Multi-Platform Transformation and Parks Super-Cycle. The Walt Disney Company (DIS) is approaching its Q2 2026 earnings report, focused on a "Dual-Engine Strategy" to scale its streaming business towards double-digit margins and aggressively expand its theme parks. Analysts currently rate DIS a "Moderate Buy" with an average target price of $133.53, driven by its content monetization, experience-driven moats, and significant parks investment. The company aims for double-digit EPS growth and 10% streaming margins by the end of 2026, while navigating competition and content costs.

Stocks to Watch This Week: AMD Reports, Disney Earns, Roblox Fights Back. This article highlights five key stocks—AMD, Apple, Broadcom, Disney, and Roblox—that are expected to drive market attention this week due to upcoming earnings reports and recent performance. Investors will be scrutinizing AMD's AI chip and data-center revenue, Apple's ability to maintain post-earnings gains, Broadcom's role in AI infrastructure, Disney's streaming profitability and theme park demand, and Roblox's user growth following safety changes. These companies represent key themes in the current market, from AI hardware to consumer spending and platform safety.

The Walt Disney Company $DIS Position Trimmed by RIA Advisory Group LLC. RIA Advisory Group LLC significantly reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 51.3% in the fourth quarter, selling 12,506 shares. Despite this trim, Disney successfully beat its quarterly earnings estimates with $1.63 EPS and a revenue increase of 5.2% year-over-year. The company faces near-term risks including an FCC review of broadcast licenses and public backlash over Marvel layoffs, while Wall Street analysts largely maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $133.53.

Sachetta LLC Decreases Stock Holdings in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Sachetta LLC significantly reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 73.8% in Q4, selling 4,899 shares and retaining 1,739 shares valued at $198,000. Despite this, Wall Street holds a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with an average target of $133.53, though the company faces rising regulatory risks due to an FCC review of its broadcast licenses and public relations challenges from Marvel layoffs. Horizon Investments LLC Decreases Stake in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. Horizon Investments LLC reduced its stake in The Walt Disney Company by 7.2% in Q4, selling 5,266 shares but still holding 67,707 shares valued at $7.70 million. The company recently surpassed quarterly earnings and revenue estimates but faces challenges including an FCC review of broadcast licenses and public criticism over Marvel layoffs.

ANGHAMI REPORTS FY2025 REVENUE OF $99.3M, UP 27%, ON 3.5M SUBSCRIBERS AND LANDMARK STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIPS. Anghami Inc. reported significant financial growth for FY2025, with revenues reaching $99.3 million, a 27% increase over the previous year. This growth was driven by subscriber gains across Anghami and OSN+, which now total 3.5 million paid subscribers and over 130 million registered users. The company also highlighted strategic partnerships, including a $57 million investment from Warner Bros. Discovery into OSN Streaming Limited and the launch of the "Epic Bundle" with Shahid and Disney+, which have expanded its content offerings and distribution channels across the MENA region.

Bath & Body Works launches an out-of-this-galaxy collection. Bath & Body Works has launched a limited-edition Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu collection to coincide with the new film's release. The collection features three new fragrances—Force Flow, Bounty Hunter, and Ice Planet—inspired by the characters and universe. Available for rewards members on May 4th and all customers on May 11th, the assortment includes body care, home fragrances, candles, and collectible accessories, extending the Disney collaboration.

Sigma Planning Corp Lowers Holdings in The Walt Disney Company $DIS. NKE: Nike vs Lululemon: Which Beaten Down Fashion Stock Can Turn It Around First?. This article analyzes Nike and Lululemon, two fashion stocks currently facing challenges, to determine which has a better chance of recovery. Nike is dealing with self-inflicted strategic missteps and is undergoing a major reset, while Lululemon is experiencing natural deceleration in its core North American market. The article concludes that Nike's stock appears undervalued with a higher potential upside due to its credible recovery plan, whereas Lululemon's stock is perceived as fairly valued, reflecting a structural repricing due to maturing growth.

Nike Criticism Over Wages And Taxes Adds To ESG Investment Risks. Nike is facing renewed scrutiny regarding its labor practices and tax contributions, as Oregon educators and Asian garment workers demand action on alleged supply chain human rights issues, wage theft, and calls for increased public education funding. This criticism adds significant environmental, social, and governance (ESG) risks for Nike, impacting its brand perception and raising questions about capital allocation amid a period of declining stock performance and existing leadership challenges. Investors are advised to monitor management's response to these allegations, as they could influence the company's long-term risk assessment and turnaround efforts.
